files.title: Overview
files.description: This is the entry point for the memo-init specification, version `v0.1.0` (Draft). It defines what the memo system is, why it exists, the guardrail philosophy that underpins it, and an index of the...

files.title: Guardrail Philosophy & Interaction Model
files.description: In agentic engineering, planning is the single most important activity. An autonomous agent can write a great deal of code quickly, but code written without a plan tends to be code that has to be...

files.title: Memo-SOP as the Canonical Entry Point
files.description: `memo-sop` is the **canonical entry skill** of the memo system. It is the one document that explains the entire process end to end: the path from a dictated transcript to an executed rollout, the...

files.title: Input Paths — Four Transcript Types
files.description: Every input to the memo system arrives as a **transcript**. The transcript server produces exactly four transcript types. The first line of the transcript — a mandatory default header — declares the...

files.title: The Five-Step Input Pipeline
files.description: Before any memo is created and before any revision is written, every input MUST pass through a five-step processing pipeline. The pipeline is mandatory and strictly sequential: no step may be...

files.title: Memo Strategies (Workflow Shape)
files.description: A memo has two independent classification dimensions. They MUST NOT be conflated.

files.title: Memo Structure & Local Guarantee
files.description: Memos are numbered with **zero-padded** identifiers (for example, `001`, `017`, `138`). Each memo lives in a directory named `{NNN}-{slug}`. The numeric prefix is stable for the life of the memo and...

files.title: Revisions & the Question Format
files.description: A revision file (`REV-XX.md`) is organized into three areas. An implementation MUST produce all three.

files.title: Phases, PRDs & the Dependency Tree
files.description: A finalized memo is decomposed in two steps.

files.title: Contamination, Empty Context and Handover
files.description: This chapter is **normative** for the contamination signal catalog, the empty-context rule, and the `HANDOVER.md` structure. The illustrative anecdotes (the "fridge memo", the contaminated handover...

files.title: Proactive Research
files.description: This chapter is **normative** for when proactive research is mandatory, where large research output is stored, and how research effort tapers across revisions. Examples are **informative**.

files.title: Quality Gates and Finalization
files.description: This chapter is **normative** for the nine finalization gates, the five quality skills, and the six evidence levels. The requirement-generated-gate wiring is **specified here but not yet implemented...

files.title: Rollout
files.description: This chapter is **normative** for the rollout trigger, the Generate→Execute→Evaluate pattern, the hands-off goal, and the standing lessons-learned file. The lessons-learned file is **specified here...

files.title: Orchestration
files.description: This chapter is **normative** for the orchestrator/agent-team roles, the state files, the crash-recovery procedure, and Task usage. The state-file schemas reflect the **real verified system**.

files.title: Agents, Skills and Tasks
files.description: This chapter is **normative** for the skill-vs-agent distinction and the first-migration boundary. The agent **migration itself is follow-up work** — adopting this chapter changes no live skill.

files.title: Prompt Generator
files.description: This chapter is **normative** for the prompt-generator contract, the three placeholder modes, and the validation discipline. It documents the **real verified engine** (177/177 tests green, zero...

files.title: Git, Security and Versioning
files.description: This chapter is **normative** for the deterministic git flow, the mandatory worktree cleanup, the `git-security` gate, and the issue rules. The worktree-cleanup enforcement is **specified here and...

files.title: Git Workflow and Memo IDs
files.description: This chapter is **normative** for the memo-ID scheme and the phase/issue/commit/PR mapping. It documents the **canonical ID schema** of the real system.

files.title: Multidimensionality
files.description: This chapter is **normative** for the multi-repo coordination scope, the project-internal default, and the no-monorepo rule.

files.title: Internal vs. External Communication
files.description: This chapter is **normative** for the boundary between inward-facing inputs and outward-facing artifacts, and for the review that an artifact MUST pass before it is published.

files.title: Workbench Sub-Spec — Overview
files.description: This is the entry point for the **workbench sub-spec** of the memo-init specification. The workbench is the meta-orchestration layer that holds all projects, their tools, and their shared...

files.title: Project Structure & Local Guarantee
files.description: This chapter is **normative** for the mandatory project layout, the local-only root, the single egress point, and the local guarantee.

files.title: Requirements Profiles (`.memo/requirements/`)
files.description: This chapter is **normative** for the requirements-profile location, the two viewpoints, the global/project split, dynamic pulling, the finalization check, and self-generated quality gates.

files.title: Tools Registry (`.memo/tools/`)
files.description: This chapter is **normative** for the tools-registry location and the checklist mechanic. The registry itself is a **RECOMMENDATION**, not a requirement.

files.title: Strands — One Memo, Many Topics
files.description: This chapter is **normative** for the strand concept, per-strand requirements and tools, and the rule that a strand is a tag rather than part of the numeric memo ID (F7=B).

files.title: Wiki Types — Project Wiki & Memo Wiki
files.description: This chapter is **normative** for the distinction between the two wiki types and for the memo-ID stamping that makes the memo wiki timeline-aware.

files.title: Trash — No Deletion, Only `.trash/`
files.description: This chapter is **normative** for the no-deletion policy, the `.trash/` target, and the prohibition on destructive removal.
